summary: Opening-construction diversity fix. Ch 13 and Ch 14 both used morning temporal frames as entry scaffolds (waking up / phone buzzing at 5:50 a.m.). Rewrote Ch 13's opening from a character-waking action to a physical-brooding action ('stared at the ceiling' instead of 'woke up'), shifting the entry point from temporal transition to spatial anchor and bodily state. Removed the downstream 'looked at the ceiling' in paragraph 4 to avoid near-redundancy with the new opening. All other seams checked clean: the three-day fight reference bridges Ch 12 correctly, the refrigerator-click motif carries across chapters, Theo's cornice drawings connect Ch 12 tail to Ch 13 interior, and the evening-to-morning transition into Ch 14 is seamless.
edits: Changed opening sentence from 'Dan woke up on a Tuesday in early May' to 'Dan stared at the ceiling on a Tuesday in early May' to avoid same morning-scaffold as Ch 14 opening
Removed 'and looked at the ceiling' from paragraph 4 to avoid redundancy with new opening image
